name: Rosy French Dressing
directions: Combine ingredients in a bowl and beat thoroughly.

name: Basic Vinaigrette Dressing With 8 Variations
directions: Shake all ingredients for your chosen variation together in a tightly-lidded container OR whisk together in a small bowl.
Let stand 10 minutes to rehydrate dried herbs and blend flavors.
Shake again then dress salad as desired.
Note -- I recommend the lemon dressing on spinach.
Note -- I recommend the Bacon Dressing, Mustard Dressing, and Balsamic Vinaigrette on strong greens such as escarole, curly endive, and dandelion greens.

Note -- The sweeter version of the mustard dressing is great for a vinegar coleslaw if you discover that you have accidentally gotten one of those occasional cabbages that are a touch on the bitter side.

Note -- feel free to play with other seasonings, more complex blends, flavored oils, and flavored vinegars. Once you've learned the technique you can customize your salad dressing to suit just about any meal.

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Shake all ingredients together in a container with a tight lid.

name: Caesar Salad
directions: Spray a cooking sheet or some layered aluminum foil with cooking spray. Place cubed bread on the sheet or foil, and give the bread a light shot of the cooking spray. Sprinkle lightly with black pepper, and place in a 375°F oven. Check every few minutes, and remove when crispy.
Peel outer leaves off lettuce and discard. Slice remaining lettuce (as a bunch) in half-inch strips. Rinse in cold water.

Peel the garlic by mashing it with the heel of your hand, the flat of a knife, or a wooden spoon --  the important thing is to get the peel off the garlic, and keep everybody happy. Take half the anchovies out of the can--the rest can be stored in the refrigerator, covered in oil, for a couple days. Microwave the lemon for thirty seconds, and then slap it around a little. Cut open CAREFULLY, squeeze out juice into bowl. Pulp is okay, but pick out the seeds with a spoon.
Combine everything except the rinsed lettuce, the oil, and the bread (should probably still be in the oven) in the blender (if you don't have a blender, whisk everything together well).

Once the mix is good and pureed, SLOWLY drizzle in the olive oil as the blender continues to run, adding a few drops at a time. The mixture should lighten, and visibly get thicker--if it doesn't, slow down (if you're not using a blender, this step will be a lot of whisking).  Add an additional egg white or a 1/4 cup of eggbeaters if the dressing is too thin.

Adjust taste with additional garlic, lemon juice, pepper, and Worcestershire sauce as desired. (Adding additional mustard will not really make the dressing taste more "caesar-y", and the dressing shouldn't need any more anchovy).
Pour mixture in salad bowl, and chill in refrigerator until ready to serve.
Add lettuce and croutons to salad bowl, toss. Garnish with Parmesan cheese and black pepper.

name: Ranch Dressing
directions: Herb Mix:
Crush parsley flakes between your palms until they are the consistency of powder.
Add all other herb mix ingredients and blend well.
Store herb mix in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid for up to 3 months. [Mine never lasts that long.].
Dressing:
Combine all ingredients and whip with a wire whisk.
Refrigerate for several hours before serving so that flavors can meld.
Allow dressing to sit out while you set-up the table. The full flavor develops better when the dressing is not refrigerator-cold.
HOWEVER, remember that you are dealing with dairy products. Refrigerate dressing as soon as the salad course is over.
Discard unused dressing according to expiration dates on your dairy products.

name: Garlic Lovers Super Caesar Salad Dressing!
directions: In a blender beat egg white, salt, pepper and sugar till frothy (about 20-30 seconds).

While the blender is still on open the hole in the blender lid, slowly drizzle in 1/2 of the vegetable oil (mixture will become much thicker).  With blender still on slowly add the rest of the ingredients (except reserved oil).  Blend until smooth.  Now once again, slowly drizzle in reserved vegetable oil while blender is on.  Once again, the dressing will become thicker.  Pour dressing into  a glass jar with at tight fitting lid.
Refrigerate for up to 1 week.

name: Easy Italian Dressing
directions: 1. In a small bowl, mix all the dry ingredients and set aside.
2. To prepare dressing, whisk together the remaining wet ingredients and 2 tablespoons of the dry.
3. Store your remaining dry ingredients in an air tight container, in your spice cabinet and use whenever you need a refill.
I double the recipe which makes 16 oz. or a regular dressing bottle full. No need to refrigerate, you can use it right away but will taste better tomorrow.
If you want to opt out of the fancy bottle, use a left over dressing bottle, and I write the wet ingredient list right on the bottle --  saves me having to save the recipe!

Serve over crisp salad greens.

name: French Dressing Even French Dressing Haters Love
directions: Blend in blender and store in an air-tight decanter in the refrigerator.  That's it!  Not sure on the serving size and the prep time is just a few minutes.  I do recommend chilling the dressing after preparation for the best taste though.

name: Red Wine Vinaigrette
directions: mix all ingredients in a jar w/lid or stir and use quickly and pour over your choice of greens.
TIP: try with leftover crispy bacon pieces on salad. Not sure why, but this dressin and bacon pieces go well on salad.

name: Garlic Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a tight sealing jar, then shake vigorously to mix.
or.
Combine all ingredients in a blender and pulse until well mixed.

name: Ginger Soy Dressing
directions: In covered jar, combine all ingredients. Shake until well blended. Cover; refrigerate until serving time, at least 2 hours.
When ready to use, shake again. Serve over salad greens or pasta salads.

name: Light Vinagrette Salad Dressing
directions: Mix dry ingredients, and pour into Mason Jar (or other Salad Dressing container).  Combine with oil and vinegar, shake well, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour prior to serving.

name: Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Whisk together all ingredients and pour over slaw.
Refrigerate and let sit about half an hour before serving.

name: Easiest Salad Dressing
directions: In a bowl, squeeze the lime juice.
Finely chop the garlic and add it to the lime juice in the bowl.
Add oil, salt, and pepper to taste.
Mix and pour over your salad.

name: Italian Salad Dressing
directions: Measure all ingredients into a small container with a tight fitting lid.
Shake vigorously to thoroughly mix.
Store in refrigerator and shake before using.

name: Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: Measure all ingredients into a small container with a tight fitting lid.
Shake vigorously to thoroughly mix.
Store in refrigerator.

name: Gibson House Asian Salad Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients in a blender, blend on high 1-2 minutes, enjoy this frothy green dressing which will keep up to 3 weeks in the fridge.

name: Yogurt Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Whisk all ingredients together.
Pour over coleslaw & mix well.
Chill for about 30 minutes to allow flavour to develop.
Store extra dressing in fridge.

name: Lemon Vinaigrette Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients and refrigerate.  Shake well before serving.

name: Spicy Soy Ginger Salad Dressing.
directions: In a medium bowl, whisk together rice wine vinegar, brown sugar, soy sauce,  Asian red chile sauce (Sriracha), and ginger. Slowly drizzle in 1/4 cup vegetable oil, whisking constantly. Season with salt to taste.

name: Summer Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in glass jar. Shake well.
Refrigerate.
Will keep several weeks in the fridge.

name: Spicy Fat Free Ranch Dressing
directions: In small bowl, mix dry mustard, salt and boiling water.  Stir and let stand a couple of minutes.  Combine dressing and mustard to taste.

name: Onion Ranch Dressing/Dip
directions: Whisk all ingredients together in a medium-size bowl until mostly smooth.
Consume immediately, or let chill in refrigerator 30 minutes for a stronger onion flavor.

name: Perfect Lemon-Garlic Dressing
directions: Whisk all ingredients until blended and light yellow or shake in jar until well combined.

name: In a Pinch Salad Dressing
directions: Mix mayo and the seasonings together. Thin with a tbls of milk at a time to get as thin or thick as you like.may add more milk if needed.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Mince garlic and press down in bowl with salt and pepper.  Whisk in lemon juice, mayo, Worcestershire and milk.  Toss in Parmesan cheese.

similar.name: Garlic Lovers Super Caesar Salad Dressing!
Caesar Dressing (Please Review Again! It's Delish, Really!)

name: Creamy French Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl.
Cover and chill until ready to serve.

similar.name: French Dressing Even French Dressing Haters Love

name: Ranch Dressing and Dip Mix
directions: In a small bowl, combine the first seven ingredients. Store in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 1 year. Yield: about 6 tablespoons mix (enough to make 6 batches).
To prepare dressing: In a bowl, combine 1 tablespoon mix with mayonnaise and buttermilk; refrigerate. Yield: 2 cups.
To prepare dip:  In a bowl, combine 1 tablespoon mix and sour cream; refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving. Yield: 1 cup.

name: Sesame Ginger Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Whisk well.
Put in refrigerator for 10-15 minutes.

name: Creamy Shallot Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients (except shallot) into a small food processor or blender and turn on machine. Blend for 30 seconds, Taste and add vinegar if needed.
Add shallot and turn machine on and off a few times until it is minced into dressing. Taste and adjust seasoning.
